title: Getting started with depictr
text: Explore data. Pick right plot pair variables:  Look model estimates. Fit model draw forest plot:  Show predictions. model predict one predictor varies?  Check fit. residual-diagnostics panel:  Convey uncertainty. Summarise posterior simulation draws:
code: explore_bivariate(crop_yield, fertilizer, yield) fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph + treatment, data = crop_yield) coefficient_plot(fit, order = "descending", title = "Drivers of crop yield") #> `height` was translated to `width`. effects_plot(fit, "fertilizer") residual_diagnostics_plot(fit) set.seed(1) draws <- data.frame(intercept = rnorm(2000, 2, 0.2),                     fertilizer = rnorm(2000, 0.012, 0.002),                     rainfall = rnorm(2000, 0.004, 0.001)) posterior_plot(draws)

title: Getting started with depictr
text: model functions built tidy_estimates(), turns model – data frame pre-computed estimates – one standard table. plotting functions also accept table, can plug estimates source (Bayesian posteriors, bootstrap intervals, numbers paper).

title: Getting started with depictr
text: theme_depictr(), depictr_palette() scale_colour_depictr() style plots :  qualitative palette based Okabe-Ito set, stays distinguishable common forms colour-vision deficiency; sequential diverging variants available . Preview :
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(crop_yield, aes(fertilizer, yield, colour = treatment)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   scale_colour_depictr() +   theme_depictr() palette_preview(type = "all")

title: Getting started with depictr
text: vignette("exploring-data") – distributions, categories, bivariate plots, scatter-plot matrices, correlations, missingness, outliers summary tables. vignette("model-estimates") – forest plots, model comparison, predicted values, interactions, random effects goodness--fit. vignette("diagnostics--uncertainty") – residuals, influence, Q-Q, ROC, calibration, confusion matrices, posteriors power curves.

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: residual_diagnostics_plot() assembles classic panel; influence_plot() qq_plot() zoom particular checks.   vif_plot() checks multicollinearity among predictors:
code: fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph, data = crop_yield) residual_diagnostics_plot(fit, title = "Crop-yield model") influence_plot(fit) vif_plot(fit)

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: binary classifiers, depictr provides ROC curve (AUC), calibration plot confusion-matrix heatmap. reads binomial glm directly, pair vectors.  positive class rare, precision-recall curve informative ROC curve:  ranking targeting tasks, cumulative gains lift charts show many positive cases captured score-ordered population targeted:
code: gfit <- glm(accuracy ~ word_frequency + RT + condition,             data = lexical_decision, family = binomial) roc_curve_plot(gfit) pr_curve_plot(gfit) gain_plot(gfit) lift_plot(gfit) calibration_plot(gfit, bins = 8) confusion_matrix_plot(gfit, threshold = 0.5, normalize = "row")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: posterior_plot() summarises draws (posterior, bootstrap, simulation) point nested intervals.
code: set.seed(2) draws <- data.frame(   stress = rnorm(2000, -0.45, 0.08),   sleep = rnorm(2000, 0.25, 0.06),   exercise = rnorm(2000, 0.15, 0.05) ) posterior_plot(draws, title = "Posterior estimates")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: power_curve_plot() reads simr::powerCurve() object tidy data frame, slow power simulation re-run redraw .
code: pc <- data.frame(nlevels = seq(10, 70, by = 10),                  mean  = c(0.16, 0.31, 0.48, 0.63, 0.78, 0.88, 0.94),                  lower = c(0.09, 0.23, 0.39, 0.54, 0.70, 0.82, 0.89),                  upper = c(0.25, 0.40, 0.57, 0.71, 0.85, 0.93, 0.97)) power_curve_plot(pc, x_lab = "Number of participants",                  title = "Power for the condition effect")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: Combine arrange_plots() save save_plot():
code: arrange_plots(   qq_plot(fit), influence_plot(fit),   ncol = 2, title = "Diagnostics", tag_levels = "A" )

title: Exploring data
text: explore_distribution() numeric variable, explore_categorical() categorical one.
code: explore_distribution(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition, type = "density") explore_categorical(wellbeing_survey, education, group = region,                     proportion = TRUE, position = "dodge")

title: Exploring data
text: explore_bivariate() picks appropriate plot automatically – scatter two numerics, boxplots numeric--categorical, filled bar chart two categoricals.  focused scatter fitted trend, use scatter_trend():
code: explore_bivariate(lexical_decision, condition, RT) scatter_trend(crop_yield, fertilizer, yield, group = treatment)

title: Exploring data
text: explore_pairs() scatter-plot matrix; correlation_heatmap() condenses information single coloured grid.
code: explore_pairs(crop_yield, cols = c("rainfall", "fertilizer", "soil_ph", "yield")) correlation_heatmap(wellbeing_survey)

title: Exploring data
text: raincloud_plot() shows density, box summary raw points together, group_comparison_plot() adds group means confidence intervals raw data – clearer answer “groups differ?” bar chart.
code: raincloud_plot(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition) group_comparison_plot(lexical_decision, RT, condition)

title: Exploring data
code: outlier_plot(crop_yield, yield) missingness_map(wellbeing_survey)

title: Exploring data
text: summary_table() builds “Table 1”: mean (SD) numeric variables, counts percentages categorical ones, optionally group. returns plain data frame, ready knitr::kable().
code: tab <- summary_table(wellbeing_survey,                      vars = c("life_satisfaction", "stress", "education"),                      group = "region") knitr::kable(tab)

title: Visualising model estimates
text: coefficient_plot() accepts model tidy_estimates() understands, tidy data frame.
code: fit <- lm(life_satisfaction ~ stress + sleep_hours + exercise_days,           data = wellbeing_survey) coefficient_plot(fit, order = "ascending",                  labels = c(stress = "Stress", sleep_hours = "Sleep (h)",                             exercise_days = "Exercise (days)"),                  title = "Predictors of life satisfaction") #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Visualising model estimates
text: compare_models() overlays estimates several models, model_fit_table() summarises fit.
code: m1 <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer, data = crop_yield) m2 <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph, data = crop_yield) m3 <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph + treatment, data = crop_yield)  compare_models(small = m1, medium = m2, full = m3, order = "descending") #> `height` was translated to `width`. knitr::kable(model_fit_table(small = m1, medium = m2, full = m3))

title: Visualising model estimates
code: freq  <- lm(life_satisfaction ~ stress + sleep_hours + exercise_days,             data = wellbeing_survey) bayes <- tidy_estimates(freq) bayes$estimate  <- bayes$estimate * 0.92      # stand-in posterior means bayes$conf.low  <- bayes$conf.low * 0.92 bayes$conf.high <- bayes$conf.high * 0.92  frequentist_bayesian_plot(freq, bayes, note_frequentist_no_prior = TRUE,                           title = "Frequentist and Bayesian estimates") #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Visualising model estimates
text: effects_plot() shows model predicts one predictor varies; interaction_plot() shows relationship changes across second predictor.   binomial glm, predictions shown probability scale:
code: fit2 <- lm(yield ~ fertilizer * treatment + rainfall, data = crop_yield) effects_plot(fit2, "rainfall") interaction_plot(fit2, "fertilizer", "treatment") gfit <- glm(accuracy ~ word_frequency + condition, data = lexical_decision,             family = binomial) effects_plot(gfit, "word_frequency")

title: Visualising model estimates
text: random_effects_plot() draws caterpillar plot. reads ‘lme4’ model directly, data frame -group estimates:
code: re <- data.frame(level = paste0("site_", 1:12),                  estimate = sort(rnorm(12, 0, 0.5)),                  std.error = runif(12, 0.1, 0.3)) random_effects_plot(re, title = "By-site departures") #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Visualising model estimates
text: mixed model fitted several optimisers (via lme4::allFit()), optimizer_fixef_plot() shows whether agree. Without ‘lme4’ can build input directly:
code: opt <- expand.grid(optimizer = c("bobyqa", "Nelder_Mead", "nlminbwrap"),                    term = c("(Intercept)", "stress", "sleep_hours")) opt$value <- c(4.5, 4.51, 4.49, -0.45, -0.44, -0.46, 0.25, 0.24, 0.26) optimizer_fixef_plot(opt)

title: Visualising model estimates
text: model_report() composes several views – coefficients, effect focal predictor, residuals-vs-fitted Q-Q plot, fit-statistics subtitle – single figure quick review report appendix.
code: full <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph + treatment,            data = crop_yield) model_report(full, title = "Crop-yield model") #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: pca_plot() runs PCA numeric columns data frame draws biplot – observations projected onto two components, variable loadings arrows. scree_plot() shows much variance component explains.   functions also accept ready-made prcomp() object, can analyse plot several views:
code: num <- c("rainfall", "fertilizer", "soil_ph", "yield") pca_plot(crop_yield, cols = num, group = "treatment",          title = "Crop-yield PCA") scree_plot(crop_yield, cols = num) pc <- prcomp(crop_yield[num], scale. = TRUE) pca_plot(pc, components = c(1, 3))

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: cluster_plot() runs k-means shows clusters first two principal components (works number variables), convex hulls labelled centroids. dendrogram_plot() draws hierarchical-clustering tree can cut k groups.
code: cluster_plot(crop_yield, cols = num, k = 3, title = "Crop-yield clusters") region_means <- aggregate(   cbind(stress, sleep_hours, life_satisfaction, age, income) ~ region,   data = wellbeing_survey, FUN = mean ) rownames(region_means) <- region_means$region dendrogram_plot(region_means[-1], k = 2, title = "Regions clustered")

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: survival_plot() draws Kaplan-Meier curves stepwise confidence limits censoring marks. estimate computed base R, modelling package needed; can pass follow-times event indicator directly, data frame, survival::survfit object.
code: set.seed(1) n <- 250 group <- sample(c("control", "treated"), n, replace = TRUE) event_time <- rexp(n, rate = ifelse(group == "treated", 0.05, 0.1)) censor_time <- runif(n, 0, 30) observed <- pmin(event_time, censor_time) event <- as.integer(event_time <= censor_time)  survival_plot(observed, event, group = group,               title = "Survival by treatment")

title: Time series
text: timeseries_plot() accepts ts object, numeric vector, data frame time, value (optionally) group columns. moving-average overlay one argument away.
code: timeseries_plot(AirPassengers, rolling = 12,                 title = "Air passengers", y_lab = "Passengers (thousands)")

title: Time series
text: acf_plot() draws autocorrelation (, type = "partial", partial autocorrelation) function, approximate significance bounds.
code: acf_plot(AirPassengers) acf_plot(AirPassengers, type = "partial")

title: Time series
text: decompose_plot() separates seasonal series trend, seasonal remainder components. Use method = "stl" (default, loess-based) method = "classical".
code: decompose_plot(AirPassengers, title = "Air passengers, decomposed")

what: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: depictr single, consistent toolkit publication-ready plots span whole analysis workflow – first look data, model estimates predictions, diagnostics, uncertainty reporting. packages one slice work, depictr aims cover end end one theme, one palette one set label conventions. Every plotting function returns ggplot2 object (patchwork composite panels), can keep customising usual + syntax. grew three focused plotting functions (frequentist_bayesian_plot, plot.fixef.allFit powercurvePlot), generalised unified one coherent package.

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pablobernabeu/depictr")

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: Explore data — explore_distribution(), explore_categorical(), explore_bivariate(), explore_pairs(), correlation_heatmap(), missingness_map(), outlier_plot(), raincloud_plot(), group_comparison_plot(), scatter_trend(), summary_table(). Multivariate, clustering & survival — pca_plot(), scree_plot(), cluster_plot(), dendrogram_plot(), survival_plot(). Time series — timeseries_plot(), acf_plot(), decompose_plot(). Model estimates & inference — tidy_estimates(), coefficient_plot(), compare_models(), frequentist_bayesian_plot(), effects_plot(), interaction_plot(), random_effects_plot(), optimizer_fixef_plot(), model_fit_table(). Diagnostics & classification — residual_diagnostics_plot(), influence_plot(), qq_plot(), vif_plot(), roc_curve_plot(), pr_curve_plot(), gain_plot(), lift_plot(), calibration_plot(), confusion_matrix_plot(). Uncertainty & power — posterior_plot(), power_curve_plot(). Theming & reporting — theme_depictr(), depictr_palette() / scale_colour_depictr(), palette_preview(), format_terms(), model_report(), arrange_plots(), save_plot(). Heavier modelling back-ends (lme4, broom, simr) optional (Suggests) used present; core functions, examples, tests vignettes run base lm/glm bundled data alone.

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
code: library(depictr)  # Explore explore_bivariate(crop_yield, fertilizer, yield) correlation_heatmap(wellbeing_survey)  # Model fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph + treatment, data = crop_yield) coefficient_plot(fit, order = "descending") effects_plot(fit, "fertilizer") interaction_plot(lm(yield ~ fertilizer * treatment, data = crop_yield),                  "fertilizer", "treatment")  # Diagnose & classify residual_diagnostics_plot(fit) gfit <- glm(accuracy ~ word_frequency + RT, data = lexical_decision,             family = binomial) roc_curve_plot(gfit)  # Report arrange_plots(qq_plot(fit), influence_plot(fit), ncol = 2, tag_levels = "A")

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data

what: How depictr relates to other packages
title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: depictr deliberately spans workflow rather competing feature--feature specialists. deep dives may still reach ggstatsplot (statistical details plots), sjPlot easystats family (see, parameters, performance), marginaleffects / ggeffects (predictions), GGally (pairs), factoextra (PCA clustering), survminer (survival), feasts / ggfortify (time series), ggdist / dabestr (distributions estimation) bayesplot / tidybayes (Bayesian). depictr gives consistent, good-looking default across tasks one package.

title: Calibration plot — calibration_plot

title: Calibration plot — calibration_plot
text: x binomial glm, vector observed outcomes. score x outcome vector, matching predicted probabilities. bins Number (equal-count) bins. colour Point/line colour. title Plot title.

title: Calibration plot — calibration_plot

title: Calibration plot — calibration_plot

title: Cluster scatter plot — cluster_plot

title: Cluster scatter plot — cluster_plot

title: Cluster scatter plot — cluster_plot
text: data data frame. cols Numeric columns cluster (default: numeric). k Number clusters k-means (ignored clusters supplied). clusters Optional vector cluster assignments (e.g. stats::kmeans() stats::cutree()); use plot clustering computed . scale Scale variables unit variance clustering PCA? hulls Draw shaded convex hull around cluster? label_centers Label cluster centroids? point_alpha Point transparency. palette Colours clusters; defaults depictr_palette(). title Plot title.

title: Forest (coefficient) plot — coefficient_plot
text: Draws horizontal point--interval ("forest") plot model estimates. input can fitted model (anything tidy_estimates() understands) data frame pre-computed estimates.

title: Forest (coefficient) plot — coefficient_plot
code: coefficient_plot(   x,   conf_level = 0.95,   intercept = FALSE,   order = c("none", "ascending", "descending"),   labels = NULL,   interaction = c("times", "asterisk", "colon", "space"),   point_colour = "#005b96",   reference_colour = "grey60",   reference_line = 0,   point_size = 2.2,   line_size = 0.7,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   x_lab = "Estimate" )

title: Forest (coefficient) plot — coefficient_plot
text: x fitted model tidy data frame estimates. conf_level Confidence/credible level, passed tidy_estimates() x model. intercept Keep intercept term? Defaults FALSE, intercept rarely interest forest plot scale often dwarfs terms. order Order terms estimate: "none" (keep input order), "ascending" "descending". labels Optional display labels terms. Either character vector length number terms (plotting order) named vector mapping raw term names labels. NULL, names tidied format_terms(). interaction Passed format_terms() control interaction terms rendered (ignored labels supplied). point_colour, reference_colour Colours estimates reference line. reference_line Position vertical reference line (e.g. 0 differences, 1 odds/risk ratios). Use NA omit . point_size, line_size Size points interval lines. title, subtitle, x_lab Plot title, subtitle x-axis label.

title: Forest (coefficient) plot — coefficient_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Forest (coefficient) plot — coefficient_plot
code: fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertilizer + soil_ph + treatment,           data = crop_yield) coefficient_plot(fit) #> `height` was translated to `width`.   # Order terms and add a title coefficient_plot(fit, order = "descending", title = "Drivers of crop yield") #> `height` was translated to `width`.

what: Compare estimates from several models or sources — compare_models
title: Compare estimates from several models or sources — compare_models
text: Overlays estimates two models (tidy estimate tables) single forest plot, one colour per source. general engine behind frequentist_bayesian_plot() (frequentist vs. Bayesian) also handy comparing nested models, several optimisers, estimates transformation.

title: Compare estimates from several models or sources — compare_models
code: compare_models(   ...,   names = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   intercept = FALSE,   order = c("none", "ascending", "descending"),   labels = NULL,   interaction = c("times", "asterisk", "colon", "space"),   dodge_width = 0.6,   reference_line = 0,   palette = NULL,   point_size = 2.2,   line_size = 0.7,   legend_title = "Source",   legend_ncol = 1,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   x_lab = "Estimate" )

title: Compare estimates from several models or sources — compare_models
text: ... Two fitted models /tidy data frames estimates. Name arguments label sources (e.g. compare_models(Frequentist = m1, Bayesian = m2)). names Optional character vector source labels, overriding names .... conf_level Confidence/credible level models. intercept Keep intercept term? Defaults FALSE. order Order terms average estimate across sources: "none", "ascending" "descending". labels Optional display labels (see coefficient_plot()). interaction Passed format_terms(). dodge_width Vertical spacing sources sharing term. reference_line Position vertical reference line (NA omit). palette Colours sources; defaults depictr_palette(). point_size, line_size Point interval-line sizes. legend_title, legend_ncol Legend title number columns. title, subtitle, x_lab Title, subtitle x-axis label.

text: modernised, self-contained successor original frequentist_bayesian_plot() gist. Rather requiring pre-built brms::mcmc_plot() object, accepts either fitted models , commonly Bayesian fits, tidy data frame posterior summaries (columns term, estimate, conf.low/conf.high, Estimate, l-95% CI, u-95% CI produced brms::fixef()). function aligns terms name, small differences naming two sources need reconciled term labels.

title: Plot frequentist and Bayesian estimates together — frequentist_bayesian_plot
code: # Same model fitted two ways here purely so the example is self-contained; # in practice `bayesian` would come from brms, rstanarm, etc. freq <- lm(life_satisfaction ~ stress + sleep_hours + exercise_days,            data = wellbeing_survey) # A stand-in "Bayesian" summary as a tidy data frame: bayes <- tidy_estimates(freq) bayes$estimate <- bayes$estimate * 0.95 frequentist_bayesian_plot(freq, bayes,                           title = "Frequentist vs. Bayesian estimates") #> `height` was translated to `width`.
